DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION FOR ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS, RESTRAINING ORDER AND TURNOVER ORDER - THE COURT NOTES THAT A MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE ULMER IN DEPT. 302 ON 8/12/16. THEREFORE, THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED TO 9/1/16 AT 9 A.M. THE COURT PRESUMES THAT IF THE MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT IS GRANTED, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL REMOVE THIS MOTION FROM THE CALENDAR FOR 9/1/16. JUDGE PRO-TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CARLOS AGUILAR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT IT ACCEPTED SIGNIFICANT PAYMENTS FROM THE PRO PER DEFENDANT WHILE THE LITIGATION WAS PENDING; THUS, DEFENDANT COULD WELL HAVE BEEN MISTAKEN ABOUT THE NEED TO DEFEND. COURT GRANTS 1- DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. THE COURT INFORMS DEFENDANT, VIA HIS SONS PRESENT, THAT THE DEADLINE TO FILE AN ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT IS AUGUST 23, 2016. MR. CARLOS AGUILAR, SON INFORMS THAT THE DEFENDANT IS ASSISTED BY AN ATTORNEY YET TO BE RETAINED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD. PREVAILING PARTY IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ryan L. Dahm of Cook Collection Attorneys PLC (415-989-4730) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Carlos Aguilar and Danny Aguilar, sons of defendant, In Pro Per Carlos Aguilar (415-291-9772) appear in place of the defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CARLOS AGUILAR'S Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Default Judgment is GRANTED. Plaintiff does not dispute that it accepted significant payments from the pro per defendant while the litigation was pending; thus, defendant could well have been mistaken about the need to defend. Court grants 1- days leave to answer. The Court informs defendant, via his sons present, that the deadline to file an Answer to the complaint is August 23, 2016. Mr. Carlos Aguilar, son informs that the defendant is assisted by an attorney yet to be retained as counsel of record. Prevailing party is directed to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with California Rule of Court 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
